Home / For Reliability Engineers

For Reliability & Maintenance Engineers

See what's failing before it fails. From the electrical panel.

Electrical Signature Analysis detects bearing wear, cavitation, misalignment, and mechanical looseness on motors, pumps, fans, and compressors. No per-asset sensors. No production shutdowns.

Get the Engineering Proof Pack Schedule a Technical Deep-Dive

One connection. Full drivetrain visibility.

Every AC motor draws a unique electrical signature. When a bearing degrades, a coupling misaligns, or cavitation develops, the signature changes. SAM4 captures these changes at the motor control cabinet. Current clamps and voltage taps connect to the MCC. No contact with the asset itself. The motor, pump, fan, gearbox, and coupling are all visible through a single measurement point.

Deep-dive into ESA technology →

Fault detection across your asset base

Bearing Degradation

Inner race, outer race, and rolling element faults detected through characteristic fault frequencies in the current spectrum.

Learn more →

Cavitation

Broadband spectral energy and flow-induced signature changes identify cavitation in centrifugal pumps before seal damage.

Learn more →

Misalignment

Angular and parallel misalignment between motor and driven equipment detected through 1x and 2x current harmonics.

Learn more →

Mechanical Looseness

Foundation looseness, soft foot, and structural resonance identified through characteristic harmonic patterns.

Learn more →

Imbalance

Mass imbalance on fans, impellers, and rotating assemblies detected through dominant 1x frequency components.

Learn more →

Electrical Faults

Stator winding degradation, rotor bar defects, and supply-side anomalies detected through voltage and current analysis.

Learn more →

Built for the assets sensors can't reach

ESA works on any AC motor-driven asset. These are the most common.

Pumps

Centrifugal, submersible, and vertical turbine. Bearing, cavitation, seal, and impeller faults.

View asset →

Fans & Blowers

Axial, centrifugal, and induced draft. Imbalance, misalignment, bearing, and belt drive.

View asset →

Compressors

Reciprocating and centrifugal. Valve, bearing, and mechanical condition.

View asset →

Conveyors

Belt, screw, and roller. Motor, gearbox, and drivetrain faults.

View asset →

Transmissions

Gearboxes and speed reducers. Gear mesh, bearing, and lubrication.

View asset →

Motors

LV and MV induction motors. Stator, rotor, and electrical supply faults.

View asset →

Verified in production. Not the lab.

0.32%

false positive rate

4–8
weeks

average early warning time

200+

industrial sites

"SAM4 detected a bearing inner race defect on a submerged pump six weeks before failure. We planned the replacement during a scheduled outage and avoided a £50K emergency intervention."

Reliability Engineer, Water Utility

See all detection stories →

What ESA can and can't do

ESA detects faults through the electrical signature. It works on any AC motor-driven asset connected to the MCC. Minimum motor size: 0.75 kW. Baseline period: 2–4 weeks of normal operation. VFD-driven motors require additional signal conditioning. ESA does not replace vibration monitoring on accessible, high-criticality assets. It extends monitoring to the assets vibration sensors cannot reach.

See full detection boundaries →

"We started with 12 pumps on a pilot. After six months and three confirmed fault detections, we expanded to 400 assets across four sites. The accuracy convinced our maintenance team."

Senior Reliability Engineer, Industrial Customer

Read full case studies →
